title: cerastium pumilum subsp. pumilum
intro: Cerastium pumilum subsp. pumilum, also known as the Alpine Chickweed, is a low-growing, perennial plant that belongs to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Cerastium pumilum subsp. pumilum include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
